title: Advania UK hiring Data Analyst in London, England, United Kingdom
snippet: Position Overview The Data Analyst will support Advania UK‚Äôs Finance team by transforming financial and operational data into trusted insight. You will build and maintain repeatable reporting and analytics solutions that improve visibility of performance (e.g., revenue, margin, cost, NRR, and forecasting KPIs), enable faster decision-making, and strengthen governance over finance data. Salary & Benefits The budgeted salary for this role is between ¬£45,000 - ¬£50,000 a year, depending on experience. Click here for the list of benefits. Hybrid working We recognise the benefits that remote and flexible working brings. We operate a hybrid working policy that allows our employees to balance time in the office and time from home. Each team within our organisation can decide how to implement this policy. If you have any questions after applying, please reach out to our recruitment team. Responsibilities Your day-to-day responsibilities will include: Partner with Finance stakeholders to understand reporting needs and translate them into clear requirements, metrics definitions, and deliverables. Develop and maintain Power BI semantic models and dashboards, including robust measures and drill-through analysis to support month-end and performance reporting. Write and optimise SQL queries to extract, join, and validate data from multiple sources; document assumptions and business logic. Build scalable data transformations and pipelines in Databricks using Python and PySpark to cleanse, standardise, and enrich finance datasets. Implement automated data quality checks (reconciliation, completeness, variance analysis) and investigate anomalies with Finance and system owners. Create efficient Excel-based analysis and templates for ad-hoc finance work (e.g., pivots, Power Query, modelling), reducing manual effort through automation where possible. Maintain clear documentation for datasets, calculations, and reporting outputs to ensure transparency and auditability. Support continuous improvement initiatives across Finance, including standardising KPIs and improving reporting cadence and self-service access. Build and maintain business-wide commercial reports and dashboards, providing insight across the full sales lifecycle (pipeline, bookings, revenue, margin, and performance KPIs). Qualifications & Experience The ideal candidate for this role will have: Essential Python experience for analysis and automation. Familiarity with Pandas library. Hands-on experience with Databricks, including PySpark for large-scale transformation and processing. Strong SQL skills for data extraction, transformation, and validation. Power BI development experience (data modelling, DAX, Power Query, report design, and performance optimisation). Desirable Experience with finance/ERP systems and data warehouses/lakehouses. Exposure to Azure data services and modern analytics platforms (e.g., Microsoft Fabric, Azure Data Lake, Synapse) Past experience of working within a finance departmen
daily_tasks:
• Power BI dashboards and semantic models with DAX measures that provide Finance stakeholders with visibility into revenue, margin, cost, and forecasting KPIs for month-end and performance reporting
• SQL queries and PySpark/Python pipelines in Databricks that extract, cleanse, and transform financial data from multiple sources into reliable, standardised datasets ready for analysis
• Automated data quality checks, reconciliation outputs, and Excel-based financial templates that flag anomalies, reduce manual effort, and ensure reporting outputs are accurate and auditable

title: Teya hiring Senior Data Analyst (Credit) in London, England, United Kingdom
snippet: Hello! We're Teya. Teya is a payment and software service provider, headquartered in London serving small, local businesses across Europe. Founded in 2019, we build easy to use, integrated tools that enable our members to accept payments and boost business performance. At Teya we believe small, local businesses are the lifeblood of our communities. We’re here because we don’t believe there’s a level playing field that gives small businesses with a fighting chance against the giants of the high street. We’re here because we see banks and legacy service providers making things harder for them. We don’t think the best technology or the best service should be reserved for those with the biggest headquarters. We’re here to fight for a future where small, local businesses can thrive, and to commit the same dedication they offer all of us. Become a part of our story. We’re looking for exceptional talent to join our mission. We offer a chance to create impact in a high-energy and connected culture, while benefiting from continuous learning opportunities, a supportive community which is proud to serve our mission, and comprehensive benefits. The Role We are looking for a Senior Credit Data Analyst to lead our data strategy within the Credit Product team. You will be responsible for the full data lifecycle — from designing robust dbt models to delivering insights that drive SMB lending decisions. You will work closely with Credit, Product, and Engineering teams to ensure our credit products are optimized, scalable, and supported by high-integrity data. Key Responsibilities Data Leadership Lead the credit business with data-driven solutions Own the roadmap for credit reporting and analytics from inception to delivery Modelling & Architecture Design, implement, and maintain scalable data models in dbt Ensure the Data Warehouse remains a reliable “source of truth” for credit performance Stakeholder Management Partner with business leads to gather requirements Translate complex raw data into well-structured data models and clear reports Self-Service Growth Develop and maintain Tableau dashboards Promote a data-driven culture by enabling non-technical stakeholders to interpret insights independently Optimization & Analysis Analyze external customer data (Credit Bureau, Companies House) alongside internal business data Improve risk assessment and product conversion performance Data Quality Maintain high data quality standards across the lifecycle Collaborate with Data Engineers to ensure reporting requirements are aligned with product implementation Must Have Requirements 3+ years’ experience in a data-related role (ideally within FinTech or Lending) Strong proficiency in SQL Strong experience with Tableau (or equivalent BI tool) Proven experience with dbt and data modelling in cloud environments (Snowflake, Redshift) Strong understanding of ETL processes Experience collaborating with Data Engineers on production pipelines Proficiency using Git for version contr
daily_tasks:
• Builds and maintains dbt data models in Snowflake that serve as the source of truth for credit performance metrics, loan book reporting, and risk assessment
• Designs and updates Tableau dashboards that allow Credit, Product, and business stakeholders to self-serve insights on SMB lending performance and product conversion
• Writes and optimises SQL queries to analyse data from internal systems and external sources (Credit Bureau, Companies House) to surface actionable findings for credit decisioning

title: VSL International Ltd. hiring Digital & Data Engineer in London, England, United Kingdom
snippet: As part of Bouygues Construction's Public Works division, VSL International participates in the construction of major technical structures and guarantees their structural safety and durability: transport infrastructure (bridges, tunnels), industrial and energy production structures, and commercial buildings. VSL's Business Is Based On Four Areas Of Expertise Structural systems and technologies Civil engineering Foundations and soil engineering Preservation, structural repairs, renovation and optimization of structures. VSL has 4,000 employees at 25 locations worldwide (Asia and Oceania, Middle East, Europe, Latin America, Canada). Position We are seeking a Digital & Data Engineer to support the rollout of digital systems and deliver robust data solutions across our ground engineering operations. This is a hybrid role combining digital implementation and data engineering, focused on improving how site data is captured, integrated, and used. You will work closely with site teams, project managers, and digital specialists to ensure accurate, timely, and structured data is available to support operational performance, commercial tracking, and data-driven decision-making. This position is ideal for an early-career to mid-level professional looking to build experience across both digital delivery and data engineering within a major infrastructure environment. Main Responsibilities Digital Rollout Deploy and configure digital tools and systems across the project (e.g. site reporting and data capture platforms) Drive adoption of digital solutions and workflows across site teams Provide training, user support, and troubleshooting to ensure effective use of systems Act as the interface between site teams and the digital team to align solutions with operational needs Monitor system usage and continuously improve digital processes based on user feedback Data Modelling & Transformation Design, develop, and maintain scalable data pipelines using Python and SQL Integrate external data sources via APIs, including authentication, extraction, and automated ingestion Transform raw data into structured, analytics-ready datasets aligned to project data models Monitor and maintain data quality across all stages (raw to processed to reporting) Implement automated workflows, including incremental loads and validation checks Ensure consistency, integrity, and reliability of data across systems Translate business and operational requirements into scalable data solutions Produce clear technical documentation covering pipelines, data models, and integrations Data Engineering Design, develop, and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports Create visualisations that clearly communicate trends, patterns, and exceptions Build automated refresh schedules, row-level security, and governance standards Ensure reports meet accessibility and usability standards for end users Data Governance & Compliance Ensure compliance with UK data protection and information governance requirements (
daily_tasks:
• Python/SQL data pipelines that extract, transform, and load site data from APIs and external sources into structured, analytics-ready datasets
• Power BI dashboards and reports that visualise operational performance, commercial tracking, and site data trends for project teams
• Technical documentation covering data models, pipeline logic, and system integrations, alongside training materials and user support for deployed digital tools

title: Haystack hiring Data Analyst in London, England, United Kingdom
snippet: Data Analyst | ¬£500 - ¬£510 We're working with a leading digital transformation consultancy delivering mission-critical public sector infrastructure on this exciting opportunity. We are searching for a technically-astute Data Analyst with a background in engineering to spearhead a major decommissioning program. You will bridge the gap between legacy codebases and modern data architecture, utilizing Python and PySpark to map complex data flows within a high-security AWS environment. The Role Lead the forensic analysis of existing systems built on Java, Node JS, and React to document end-to-end data lineage. Reverse engineer complex data models and system dependencies to support critical decommissioning and migration decisions. Use Python, PySpark, and AWS Glue to validate data pipelines and confirm how information transforms across the wider program. Ensure data integrity and accuracy by implementing rigorous testing frameworks using PyTest and Great Expectations. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to clarify ownership, integration points, and RDS storage structures. What You'll Need An active SC Clearance is mandatory due to the secure nature of the environment. Strong technical background with the ability to read and understand Java, Node JS, and React codebases. Advanced proficiency in Python, PySpark, and working with JSON/RDS datasets. Proven experience in Cloud environments (AWS preferred, Databricks is a significant plus). Expertise in Data Quality management and automated testing (PyTest, Great Expectations). What's On Offer Competitive day rate of ¬£500 - ¬£510 (Inside IR35). 100% Remote working flexibility for a better work-life balance. Long-term stability with a 1-year contract duration. The chance to work on large-scale, impactful datasets within a high-profile secure program. Apply via Haystack today!
daily_tasks:
• Data lineage documentation mapping end-to-end data flows across legacy Java, Node JS, and React codebases
• Validated data pipeline reports confirming how information transforms across the program using Python, PySpark, and AWS Glue
• Data quality test results and integrity checks produced through automated testing frameworks using PyTest and Great Expectations

title: Checkout.com hiring Graduate, Data Science & Analytics in London, England, United Kingdom
snippet: Company Description We’re Checkout.com – you might not know our name, but companies like eBay, ASOS, Klarna, Uber Eats, and Sony do. That moment when you check out online? We make it happen. Checkout.com is where the world checks out. Our global network powers billions of transactions every year, making money move without making a fuss. We spent years perfecting a service most people will never notice. Because when digital payments just work, businesses grow, customers stay, and no one stops to think about why. With 19 offices spanning six continents, we feel at home everywhere – but London is our HQ. Wherever our people work their magic, they’re fast-moving, performance-obsessed, and driven by being better every day. Ideal. Because a role here isn’t just another job; it’s a career-defining opportunity to build the future of fintech. Job Description As a Data Analytics Engineer at Checkout you will be responsible for enabling key insights on how products are performing and establishing a single source of truth for North Star and tracking metrics, working closely with product managers and product data scientists to shape the product’s evolution at Checkout. You'll have the opportunity to build new data products and introduce step changes in how we view analytics for these critical areas. You'll have end-to-end ownership of multiple data products from design to implementation to the operationalisation. How You’ll Make An Impact Design and implement high-performance, reusable, and scalable data models for our data warehouse using dbt and Snowflake Design and implement Looker structures (explores, views, etc) which will enable users across the organization to self-serve analytics Work closely with data analysts and business teams to understand business requirements and provide data ready for analysis and reporting Continuously discover, transform, test, deploy and document data sources and data models Apply, help define, and champion data warehouse governance: data quality, testing, documentation, coding best practices and peer reviews Take initiative to improve and optimise analytics engineering workflows and platforms Key Requirements Proven delivery experience as a data, business intelligence or analytics engineer Hands-on proven data modeling and data warehousing skills demonstrated in large-scale data environments Proven experience in software development lifecycle in analytics (e.g. version control, testing, and CI/CD) Excellent SQL and data transformation skills (e.g. ideally proficient in dbt or similar) Familiarity with at least one of these Cloud technologies: Snowflake, AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure Passionate about sales, finance, customer, marketing and/or product analytics data Good attention to detail to highlight and address data quality issues Additional Information Bring all of you to work We create the conditions for high performers to thrive, through real ownership, fewer blockers, and work that makes a difference from day on
daily_tasks:
• Scalable data models built in dbt and Snowflake that serve as a single source of truth for product and business metrics
• Looker explores and views that enable self-serve analytics reporting across business teams
• Documented, tested, and version-controlled data pipelines that transform raw data sources into analysis-ready datasets

title: Immediate hiring Data Engineer in London, England, United Kingdom
snippet: Data Engineer (up to £55,000) Immediate is home to some of the biggest and most loved consumer brands in the UK, including Radio Times, Good Food and BBC Gardeners World magazine. Our trusted, quality content reaches millions of people a month across digital, print, video, podcasts, apps and live events.  We're the destination for people looking to get more from the things they love. We're here to inspire, fuel, encourage and educate.  Whether it's the big things, or the little things; from reaching fitness goals to savouring good food, cultivating outdoor spaces, or enjoying the latest TV sensation - we help bring our audiences joy!  About the team  The Data Engineering Team is a highly skilled and effective team, that is in high demand within the business. The team works within the Product & Technology department at Immediate Media to deliver multi-department projects and service the data requirements of the business.  About you   You will have technical experience and a passion for solving data problems. You will be comfortable handling data in any format, data modelling and ETL processes. You will have a demonstrable technical skillset as data engineering skills are paramount to this role.  You will have good communication skills and be comfortable working with stakeholders and translating business requirements into technical specifications.  You should be delivery driven, self-motivated and capable of independent working. You should strive for best practice and technical excellence and be a person that actively looks for continual improvement.  As a Data Engineer, you will; Develop data solutions to meet company needs.  Build robust data pipelines to get data where it needs to be.  Take ownership for delivering engineering tasks on time and to specification.  Build strong relationships with key stakeholders outside the team.  Be responsible for data quality and ensuring problems are resolved swiftly.  Contribute to robust processes and technical best practice.  Help maintain and improve the data platform and technology.  Foster skills, share knowledge and cross-train with peers.  Requirements Experience as a Data Engineer, Analytics, Software Engineer or Technical Analyst.  Good knowledge of Python including using Pyspark/Apache Spark.  Proven experience building and maintaining data pipelines end-to-end.  Advanced SQL skills.  Knowledge of one or more data warehouse or lakehouse platforms such as Snowflake, Databricks, MS Synapse.  Experience with a cloud platform (Azure/GCP/AWS)  Experience with Code control/Git  Have used an orchestration tool, such as ADF, Airflow, Glue or Data Factory  Advantageous, but not required:  Databricks  DBT  CI\CD  Atlassian (Jira, Confluence)  Benefits A relaxed working environment with regular socials including a summer festival Supportive well-being initiatives and benefits, talks & workshops, and Mental Health First aiders & Champions 25 days holiday plus a day for your birthday. Our offices will be closed

• Writes and optimises Python and SQL code to develop data solutions that meet business requirements, ensuring data quality and accuracy throughout
• Creates and documents technical specifications translated from stakeholder business requirements, contributing to data platform improvements and engineering best practices

title: Haystack hiring Data Analyst in London, England, United Kingdom
snippet: Data Analyst | £44,290 - £47,800 We're working with a major high-impact enterprise organisation managing a complex, large-scale digital ecosystem on this exciting opportunity. We are looking for a data detective who can bridge the gap between technical infrastructure and operational excellence by ensuring seamless data flow across CRM, HR, and Finance platforms. This isn't a typical dashboards-and-reporting role; you will be the primary troubleshooter for business-critical integrations using SQL to interrogate relational databases and solve complex data mapping puzzles. If you enjoy hunting down root causes in complex data environments, this is the challenge for you. The Role Lead the investigation into data discrepancies and failed integrations across a multi-system enterprise landscape. Perform deep-dive analysis on source and target systems to understand complex mapping and transformation logic. Partner with diverse stakeholders to resolve data quality issues at the source, ensuring long-term integration reliability. Monitor ongoing data health and develop strategies for reconciliation across platforms like CRM, learning systems, and financial tools. Act as the data subject matter expert within a specialist integration team, focusing on data integrity rather than software development. What You'll Need Expert-level SQL skills with extensive experience querying relational databases such as SQL Server, Oracle, or MySQL. Proven track record of troubleshooting data issues within an ETL or integrated system environment. Strong understanding of data mapping, transformation logic, and complex reconciliation processes. Experience working within large-scale organisations (e.g., Public Sector, Finance, Education, or Healthcare) managing multiple stakeholder groups. A methodical, persistent, and detail-oriented approach to problem-solving and root-cause analysis. What's On Offer Competitive salary up to £47,800 plus a generous pension scheme and annual leave allowance. Rapid professional development opportunities within a technically challenging and supportive environment. Hybrid working model based in a prime London location (2 days per week onsite). 12-month fixed-term contract with the potential to make a visible impact on large-scale digital operations. Apply via Haystack today!
daily_tasks:
• SQL query outputs and investigation reports that identify root causes of data discrepancies and failed integrations across CRM, HR, and Finance platforms
• Data reconciliation analyses comparing source and target systems to validate mapping and transformation logic accuracy
• Data health monitoring documentation and reconciliation strategies that ensure ongoing integration reliability across enterprise platforms

title: Bloomberg hiring Statistical Data Analysis Trainer in London, England, United Kingdom
snippet: Location London Business Area Data Ref # 10048138 Description & Requirements Bloomberg runs on data. Our products are fueled by powerful information. We combine data and context to paint the whole picture for our clients, around the clock – from around the world. In the Data department, we are responsible for delivering this data, news, and analytics through innovative technology — quickly and accurately. We apply problem-solving skills to identify innovative workflow efficiencies and implement technology solutions to enhance our systems, products, and processes — all while providing platinum customer support to our clients. The Team At Bloomberg, our team is responsible for onboarding our junior data engineers, as well as providing learning opportunities to develop the skills of our nearly 2,000 Data employees. We collaborate with all teams across Data to ensure that we deliver the highest quality educational development. We also roll up our sleeves to create our own training and applied exercises. You can support our purpose by preparing Data teams for an AI-driven future by strengthening their analytical reasoning, statistical literacy, and confidence in applying AI tools responsibly and effectively. We strive to make our curriculum exciting for both trainers and trainees; we use interactive technology, peer learning, and a highly collaborative team culture to ensure success for everyone. We encourage participation and provide opportunities for trainees to learn from each other and the professionals within Data. We’ll Trust You To Design and deliver training on applied experimentation and causal reasoning that enables teams to evaluate process changes - such as adopting new data pipelines, switching validation methods, or implementing AI-assisted workflows - and quantify their impact on dataset quality and business outcomes. Build a curriculum on experimental design, A/B testing, and hypothesis testing for data operations and teach teams to run controlled experiments to quantify improvements based on workflow changes Design and deliver analytics and statistics training that strengthens quantitative reasoning, data quality assessment (accuracy, completeness, reliability), and AI-enhanced insight generation. Create hands-on labs where teams design experiments on real Bloomberg datasets—testing pipeline changes, evaluating new tools, and measuring quality improvements using statistical methods. Explain core statistical concepts (sampling, correlation, causation, p-values) in the context of data quality and process optimization. Incorporate AI-assisted tools (e.g., GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, NotebookLM) into training design and delivery. Ensure teams maintain the highest standards for data quality, observability, and governance, alongside the implementation of transformative AI technologies. Create structured guides and reusable frameworks (experiment templates, statistical calculators, decision tools) that enable teams to independently design exper
daily_tasks:
• Designs and delivers hands-on training sessions and labs where data teams run controlled experiments, A/B tests, and hypothesis tests on real Bloomberg datasets to measure workflow and quality improvements

• Builds reusable curriculum materials including experiment templates, statistical calculators, decision tools, and structured guides that teams can independently use after training

• Develops and updates course content incorporating AI-assisted tools (e.g., GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT) to teach statistical concepts such as sampling, p-values, and causal reasoning in the context of data quality and process optimization

title: Haystack hiring Data Engineer in London, England, United Kingdom
snippet: Data Engineer | £80,000 - £90,000 We're working with a fast-growing energy-tech innovator at the forefront of the global renewables transition on this exciting opportunity. As the world moves away from fossil fuels, you will build the high-performance data infrastructure that optimizes large-scale battery storage and renewable assets. In this role, you’ll leverage a modern stack including Python, AWS, and Terraform to deliver robust data products that power real-time asset telemetry and market analytics. We are looking for a technical leader who wants to combine deep engineering craft with a massive green-energy impact. The Role Design, build, and scale mission-critical data services and ETL pipelines using Python and Prefect to manage complex energy market data. Architect and deploy cloud-native infrastructure on AWS (S3, Athena) using Terraform for Infrastructure as Code (IaC). Enable advanced analytics and asset optimization by integrating real-time data streams with Pulsar and high-performance databases like Cassandra and PostgreSQL. Lead DevOps best practices within the data team, utilizing Docker and GitLab CI/CD to ensure seamless, high-frequency deployments. Mentor junior engineers and champion data quality, reliability, and MLOps principles across the engineering organization. What You'll Need Advanced Python programming skills with significant experience building and deploying production-level data services from scratch. Proven expertise in modern data engineering, including deep knowledge of SQL/NoSQL databases (PostgreSQL, Cassandra) and API design. Strong hands-on experience with the AWS ecosystem, specifically managing data lakes and analytics tools like Athena. Solid understanding of containerization (Docker) and automated CI/CD pipelines to maintain a fast-paced development lifecycle. Excellent collaborative skills with the ability to bridge the gap between Data Science, Engineering, and commercial stakeholders. What's On Offer Competitive salary (£80k - £90k) with annual performance and salary reviews. Modern flexible working environment with a hybrid London office and enhanced parental leave. Comprehensive growth support including a dedicated learning & development budget and paid professional memberships. Vibrant company culture featuring inclusive employee networks, regular socials, and seasonal festive closures. Apply via Haystack today!
daily_tasks:
• Production-ready ETL pipelines and data services built in Python and Prefect that ingest, transform, and deliver complex energy market and asset telemetry data
• Cloud infrastructure code written in Terraform that provisions and maintains AWS resources (S3, Athena) as repeatable, version-controlled deployments
• CI/CD pipeline configurations and containerised application builds using Docker and GitLab that enable frequent, reliable releases of data products to production

title: OakNorth hiring DATA ANALYST, MARKETING in London, England, United Kingdom
snippet: About OakNorth At OakNorth, we’re on a mission to empower the UK’s most ambitious businesses. Since 2015, we’ve lent over $21 billion across the UK and US, helped create more than 58,000 new homes and 36,000 new jobs, and supported hundreds of thousands of personal savers, all while fuelling the UK economy. As OakNorth continues to grow across multiple products and customer segments, clear, effective communication and rigorous measurement are critical. Marketing plays a central role in how we explain value, drive adoption and support commercial performance across the bank. The role We’re looking for a Marketing Data Analyst to join the Marketing team and enable growth across OakNorth’s Lending, Business Banking and Personal Savings products through actionable insights, data-driven strategies and robust measurement frameworks. This is a hands-on analytics role, central to how marketing and commercial performance is understood and optimised. You will work closely with Marketing, Product, Commercial and Finance teams to deliver commercial intelligence, performance analysis and platform optimisation that improve unit economics, sharpen go-to-market activity and accelerate growth. You will own key elements of marketing data and analytics, from attribution and reporting to experimentation and insight generation, helping teams understand what is working, where to invest and how to scale effectively. What You'll Do Marketing analytics and data management Own marketing analytics and insight generation, partnering with MarkOps on data quality and enrichment, and performing targeted cleansing as needed to enable accurate analysis and visualisation. Define and influence the build and maintenance of attribution logic and performance benchmarks to support confident decision making. Analyse channel efficiency, conversion metrics and customer behaviour to improve marketing efficiency and unit economics. Design, execute and analyse experiments and A/B tests using sound statistical principles. Leverage automation and AI-driven approaches to improve insight generation, productivity and scalable marketing execution. Platforms, tools and systems Work hands on to analyse, model and visualise marketing performance data. Build and maintain dashboards and self-serve reporting using visualisation tools such as Looker, Tableau or similar. Use SQL extensively to query, transform and analyse large, complex datasets. Work confidently with Excel for analysis, modelling and validation alongside SQL-based workflows. Support Marketing Operations team with CRM platforms, particularly Salesforce, to support marketing performance tracking and funnel analysis. Use GA4 and related digital analytics tools to understand acquisition behaviour, channel performance and conversion. Partner closely with Data Engineering and technical teams to improve pipelines, models and reporting infrastructure. Cross-functional collaboration and enablement Act as a strategic partner to Marketing, Commerci

title: SLR Consulting hiring Data Analyst in London, England, United Kingdom
snippet: The Responsible Finance team within SLR advises the world’s most successful investors on responsible investment. The Responsible Finance team assists global asset management firms in establishing best-in-class environmental, social, and governance (ESG) programs that are deployed throughout the investment lifecycle in order to mitigate risk and create value. We partner with firms that invest across a range of industries, including financial services, healthcare, technology, manufacturing, and consumer products. The Responsible Finance team was an early entrant into this rapidly-evolving industry and continues to be at the forefront of ESG innovation and defining best practices. Job Description The Data Analyst will report to the Data Lead and support data advisory work across Responsible Finance division. Responsible Finance data projects consist primarily of developing a tailored data strategy, collecting, validating, analyzing, and reporting on a private market investor client’s portfolio data. Sample projects include building and facilitating industry-specific data collection surveys, conducting annual trend analyses on portfolios of 15-50 companies, creating investor presentations on year-over-year portfolio performance, and visualizing data in interactive dashboards. The Data Analyst should cultivate an understanding of global private and public market ESG data frameworks and standards, including EDCI, SFDR, SASB, TCFD, etc.  Responsibilities  Execute Responsible Finance client projects, including supporting strategy creation, data collection, validation, analysis, and reporting, typically supporting on 3-5 overlapping projects in a given week. Development of ESG data collection surveys, tailored to GP client needs, industry frameworks (e.g., EDCI, SFDR), and sectoral materiality. Coordinating with GP client’s portfolio companies to support accurate and timely data collection, leading email coordination and eventually supporting on live calls. Develop validation tools and processes to ensure data quality. Analyze data trends and compile insights into client deliverables like reports and data packs Cultivate understanding of core ESG data frameworks (EDCI, SFDR, TCFD, etc.) and global Limited Partner (LP) expectations. Support with ongoing evolution and improvements of SLR’s Responsible Finance data offerings through process efficiency improvements, training materials, internal best practice-sharing, and insight delivery. Leverage a range of internal and third-party data tools to complete Responsible Finance data projects and be a resource to broader team on the integration of these tools. About Us  SLR are global leaders in Sustainability Solutions, helping our clients achieve their sustainability goals. title: Fussy hiring Senior Data Analyst in London, England, United Kingdom
snippet: About Fussy Hey good smelling! We’re Fussy…about everything in our mission to banish single-use plastic from your bathroom. Having experienced exceptional growth over the past four years, we are now looking for a talented individual to join the team as Data Analyst. You’ll report to Mary, our Finance Director and work together to support and empower users across the business to use data to unlock insights. This is an exciting opportunity to join a scale-up through a significant period of growth. You’ll be a trusted partner to the leadership team and a champion for data led decision making! 💪🌍 About The Role Embedded with teams (Growth, Marketing, Ops) to understand their goals and translate business questions into technical data requirements and analysis. Design, develop and maintain reporting dashboards to track key business KPIs (Experience with DTC/E-commerce metrics: LTV, CAC, Retention Cohorts, and Attribution models) Becoming an expert on our data and visualisation tool and using your knowledge to upskill others Own, maintain and improve our data stack alongside our analytics engineer Ensure the reliability and integrity of our data across all touch points, keeping data flowing to all business users. Working with department leads to define and standardize KPIs, ensuring everyone is looking at a Single Source of Truth. While your primary focus will be on delivery and stakeholder alignment, you will work closely with our analytics engineer to contribute to our modern data stack, leveling up your technical stack toward Analytics Engineering. About You We’re big believers that great talent comes in all shapes, sizes and CVs. So if you’re excited by our mission, we want to hear from you. That said, we think you’ll smash this role if you bring the following skills: Essential At least 2–3 years experience in a dedicated Data or BI Analyst role, ideally within e-commerce, DTC, or omni-channel businesses. BI Tool Proficiency: (We use ThoughtSpot) Advanced experience with modern BI platforms (e.g., Looker/Look ML, ThoughtSpot, Tableau). You should know how to build more than just "pretty charts"—you will need to build functional, interactive tools. Stakeholder Management: Proven track record of translating vague business questions into concrete data requirements and KPIs. Commercial curiosity with the ability to link data insights to business outcomes Excel/Google Sheets: Still a staple for quick-turnaround ad-hoc analysis. SQL: Familiar using SQL to analyse data and draw insights Nice to have Cloud Data Warehousing: Familiarity with one of Snowflake, BigQuery, or Redshift. Analytics Engineering Exposure: Basic understanding of dbt or version control (Git) The Modern Data Stack (MDS): Awareness of how data moves from source to warehouse to BI (Fivetran, Airbyte, etc.). title: Ealing Council (London Borough of Ealing) hiring Corporate Data Analyst in London, England, United Kingdom
snippet: £52,194 - £54,267 pa inclusive Grade 12 2 Years Fixed Term Contract or Secondment Opportunity for LBE Staff Ealing Council has an exciting opportunity for a Corporate Data Analyst on a full‑time basis. We are committed to delivering value for money for residents by directing investment to where it delivers the greatest impact. The Medium Term Financial Strategy (MTFS) Programme Management Office (PMO) supports the MTFS Board by overseeing the delivery of projects and programmes that strengthen the Council’s long‑term financial sustainability. The PMO monitors and reports on the delivery of agreed savings from the 2026/27 budget and identifies further opportunities to support financial sustainability in 2027/28 and 2028/29. Through effective resource management and a focus on prevention, we aim to deliver sustainable services and improved outcomes for residents. As a Corporate Data Analyst in the MTFS Board PMO, you will lead analytical work that supports strategic commissioning and transformation across the organisation. Working closely with the Assistant Director for Commissioning and Capacity Building, you will play a key role in enabling data driven decision making to implement the medium term financial strategy and transition to an asset-based commissioning model that empowers Connected Communities. You will ensure data is effectively prepared, managed, and analysed to generate insights that align with organisational priorities. You will guide best practices across teams, influence strategic direction through evidence, and ensure ethical and impactful use of data. Our ideal Corporate Data Analyst will have the below skills and experience: Proficient in modern data visualisation tools such as Power BI and/or Tableau, with advanced skills in MS Excel, MS Access, and Business Objects. Skilled in analytical programming languages including Python, R, and SQL, with the ability to design, test, and document robust analytical solutions. Experienced in handling large and complex datasets: extracting, cleansing, linking, and validating data from diverse sources including databases, spreadsheets, and ICT systems. Demonstrates advanced analytical and evaluation capabilities, with a strong grasp of quantitative analysis, forecasting methodologies, and the ability to interpret both statistical and written information. Able to develop systems and models tailored to organisational challenges using appropriate data tools and methodologies. Skilled in presenting data insights through dashboards and visualisations that support strategic decision-making and stakeholder engagement. Adept at identifying opportunities for data driven products and solutions that enable transformation and enhance service delivery. Experienced in implementing data governance frameworks, improving data quality, and promoting safe and ethical data use aligned with organisational values and commissioning principles. Confident in translating complex and technical data into clear, actionab
